[단독] 트럼프 행정부, 앤스로픽(Anthropic) '페이블(Fable)' 모델에 대한 제한 해제  트럼프 행정부가 이르면 화요일 저녁(현지시각), 미국 기술 기업 앤스로픽(Anthropic)의 최첨단 AI 모델인 '페이블 5(Fable 5)'에 대한 수출 제한을 해제할 예정입니다. 이로써 최근 몇 주간 접근이 차단되었던 해당 AI 모델을 일반 사용자들이 다시 사용할 수 있게 될 전망입니다.  페이블 5(Fable 5)의 특징: 페이블 5는 앤스로픽의 강력한 '미토스 5(Mythos 5)' 모델의 소비자용 버전으로, 사이버 공격에 악용되는 것을 방지하기 위한 강력한 안전장치를 갖추고 있습니다.  수출 제한과 반발: 행정부는 지난 6월 12일, 사이버 보안을 우려해 페이블과 미토스 모델의 접근을 차단하는 수출 통제 조치를 내렸습니다. 하지만 이는 미국 AI 업계와 유럽 동맹국들의 큰 우려를 낳았습니다. 특히 유럽 국가들은 적국보다 먼저 자국 컴퓨터 네트워크의 취약점을 찾아내기 위해 해당 모델의 사용을 원해왔습니다. (미토스 모델에 대한 제한은 지난 금요일 늦게 일부 해제되었습니다.)  행정부의 딜레마: 이번 제한 해제 조치는 첨단 AI 모델이 가진 사이버 보안 위험을 통제하는 동시에, 중국과의 치열한 경쟁 속에서 자국 AI 산업의 혁신을 저해하지 않아야 하는 행정부의 균형 찾기 노력을 보여줍니다.  AI 업계의 우려와 불만: 최근 경쟁사인 오픈AI(OpenAI) 역시 백악관의 압박으로 인해 챗GPT-5.6(ChatGPT-5.6) 모델을 소수의 특정 그룹에게만 제한적으로 제공해야 했습니다. 샘 알트만 오픈AI 최고경영자(CEO) 등 업계 관계자들은 정부의 이러한 즉흥적이고 예측 불가능한 규제 방식에 불만을 표하고 있습니다.  향후 전망: AI 업계 경영진들은 조만간 발표될 첨단 AI 모델 평가 관련 행정명령을 통해, 이러한 결정에 대한 명확한 프로세스가 확립되고 정책적 불안정성이 해소되기를 기대하고 있습니다.

드디어 Claude Somnet 5가 출시 되었다.🎉  특징만 간단히 보자면 다음과 같다.  🔹 가격 대비 성능 오퍼스4.8에 근접한 성능을  훨씬 낮은 가격에 제공한다.  출시가는 입력 $2 / 출력 $10  (8/31까지, 이후 $3/$15)  🔹 에이전틱 능력 강화 역대 Sonnet 중 가장  강력한 자율 작업 수행력을 가짐. 브라우저, 터미널 도구 사용,  다단계 계획 실행이 가능함.  🔹 코딩 실제 PR 작업을 끝까지 처리.  버그 재현 테스트 작성→수정→검증까지  한 번에 끝냄  🔹 안전성 소넷4.6 대비 환각, 아첨 비율 낮아짐.  사이버 보안 위험은  오퍼스보다 현저히 낮게 통제됨  🔹 무료/프로 플랜 기본 모델로 적용.  클로드 코드, API 동시 지원

NFSC聯盟譯統任務2.9.9.9. 2026년 6월 19일 연맹 특별 생방송 가바드, 바이러스 진실 폭로 / 중공, 자본 유출 차단 장벽 구축 이번 공개는 단순한 정보 공개를 넘어 역사적 의미를 지닌 중요한 기록이다. 과거 여러 논란과 쟁점이 공식 문서와 기록 형태로 정리되어 남겨졌기 때문이다. 그동안 법치재단, 법치사회 그리고 많은 전우들은 바이러스 기원 관련 정보와 진실을 알리기 위해 지속적으로 노력해 왔다. 그러나 당시 일부 언론과 평론가들은 이러한 주장들을 음모론으로 규정하며 받아들이지 않았다. 최근 공개되는 각종 문서는 팬데믹의 기원, 정보 전달 과정, 책임 규명 문제에 대해 대중이 다시 살펴보도록 만들고 있다. 관련 논의는 지금도 계속 확산되고 있다.

sample_tweets.text: 🧪 ForensicsTools – 2,000+ DFIR Resources, Tools, Labs, and Learning Materials in One Repository  ForensicsTools is a comprehensive open-source repository that curates hundreds of free digital forensics and incident response (DFIR) tools, distributions, frameworks, training resources, CTFs, books, datasets, and reference materials for forensic investigations across Windows, Linux, macOS, mobile devices, networks, cloud environments, browsers, memory, and disk images. The collection includes industry-standard tools such as Autopsy, The Sleuth Kit, Volatility, Plaso, Velociraptor, RegRipper, Wireshark, ExifTool, Hashcat, FTK Imager, Guymager, Timesketch, MemProcFS, and many others, organized by forensic discipline including memory analysis, artifact extraction, timeline analysis, metadata analysis, steganography, acquisition, imaging, browser forensics, network investigations, mobile forensics, and malware analysis. In addition to tooling, it provides extensive educational resources, forensic challenges, certification references, blogs, datasets, and research material, making it an excellent starting point for DFIR professionals, SOC analysts, incident responders, malware analysts, digital forensic investigators, cybersecurity students, and anyone building practical forensic capabilities.  🔗 github.com/mesquidar/Forensi… #DFIR #DigitalForensics #IncidentResponse #CyberSecurity #BlueTeam #MalwareAnalysis #MemoryForensics #OpenSource #SOC #ThreatHunting

Massive DFIR collections like this are useful for discovery but only if someone actively maintains them. Tools go dead, distributions get abandoned, links rot. Worth checking commit history before treating it as a reliable reference. The mix of tools plus training materials plus datasets plus CTFs is what makes it more than a link dump. DFIR is a field where practicing on real data matters way more than reading about techniques, so having challenge sets alongside the toolchain gives people an actual path from beginner to competent.

📧 User Scanner – Advanced Email & Username OSINT Intelligence Suite  User Scanner is a powerful open-source OSINT framework designed to perform deep reconnaissance using only an email address or username. It combines email intelligence and username enumeration into a single modular platform capable of scanning more than 290 online services, including 105+ email-supported platforms and 185+ username-based services. Rather than simply checking account availability, User Scanner extracts publicly accessible profile metadata such as usernames, profile details, account statistics, avatars, registration status, and other digital footprint indicators to help investigators build a broader picture of an online identity. The framework supports single-target investigations, bulk scanning from input files, username permutation generation, category-specific searches, modular platform selection, proxy rotation with health validation, JSON/CSV exports, and developer-friendly Python library integration. It also integrates with Hudson Rock threat intelligence to determine whether a username or email appears in known infostealer malware logs, making it useful for incident response and credential exposure investigations. User Scanner is intended for authorized OSINT investigations, cyber threat intelligence, digital investigations, asset discovery, and defensive security research using publicly available information.  🔗 github.com/kaifcodec/user-sc… #CyberSecurity #OSINT #ThreatIntelligence #DigitalForensics #ThreatHunting #Reconnaissance #EmailOSINT #OpenSource #DFIR

One cybersecurity lesson that really stood out to me this week:  The most valuable thing an attacker steals isn't always your password, it's your identity.  Modern infostealers target:  • Browser cookies • Session tokens • Saved credentials • Authentication data That's why simply changing a password may not be enough after a compromise. As I learn more about SOC operations, one idea keeps coming up: Good defenders don't investigate alerts in isolation.  They correlate:  • Endpoint telemetry • Authentication logs • DNS activity • VPN events • Cloud audit logs  Every additional data source adds context.  Every piece of context improves detection.  That's the mindset I'm trying to build as I transition from software engineering into cybersecurity. #CyberSecurity #BlueTeam #SOC #ThreatDetection #IdentitySecurity #DFIR

🤖 Sec-Gemini – Google's Experimental AI SDK & CLI for Cybersecurity Defenders  Sec-Gemini is Google's experimental AI platform built specifically for cybersecurity professionals, security researchers, SOC analysts, DFIR teams, and defenders. Unlike general-purpose AI assistants, Sec-Gemini is designed to understand cybersecurity concepts, assist with threat analysis, incident response, malware investigation, vulnerability research, and defensive security workflows. The open-source project provides Python and TypeScript SDKs, a command-line interface (CLI), and a web component that allows developers to integrate Sec-Gemini directly into security applications, automation pipelines, internal tools, and web platforms. Security teams can use it to streamline cyber investigations, automate repetitive analysis tasks, accelerate incident response, and build AI-powered security workflows without creating their own LLM infrastructure. The project also includes notebooks, OpenAPI specifications, and integration examples, making it suitable for building AI-assisted SOC tooling, DFIR automation, security chat interfaces, and custom cybersecurity applications. While the SDKs and CLI are open source, the underlying Sec-Gemini model remains an experimental Google cybersecurity AI service intended to augment human defenders rather than replace traditional security tools.  🔗 github.com/google/sec-gemini #CyberSecurity #AI #SecGemini #Google #ThreatIntelligence #SOC #DFIR #IncidentResponse #SecurityAutomation #OpenSource

🎓 Awesome Incident Response – Curated DFIR Resources, Tools, and Playbooks for Security Teams  Awesome Incident Response is a comprehensive open-source collection of tools, frameworks, playbooks, knowledge bases, books, training resources, and reference material for Digital Forensics and Incident Response (DFIR). It brings together hundreds of resources covering the complete incident response lifecycle, including evidence collection, endpoint triage, memory forensics, log analysis, malware investigation, timeline reconstruction, disk imaging, threat hunting, adversary emulation, case management, automation, and incident coordination. The repository features widely adopted tools such as Velociraptor, GRR Rapid Response, Autopsy, The Sleuth Kit, Volatility, Sigma, Chainsaw, Hayabusa, Security Onion, SIFT Workstation, CAINE, IRIS, TheHive, FleetDM, Osquery, and many others. In addition to tooling, it provides curated books, knowledge bases, Linux forensic distributions, training material, and community resources, making it an excellent reference for SOC analysts, DFIR investigators, incident responders, threat hunters, malware analysts, blue teams, and cybersecurity students building practical incident response capabilities.  🔗 github.com/meirwah/awesome-i… #IncidentResponse #DFIR #DigitalForensics #BlueTeam #ThreatHunting #SOC #CyberSecurity #ThreatDetection #OpenSource

The second body was found in the Purna River, around 22 km from the incident site. Ravindra Rambhau Aware and Gopal Vasudev Nirvan, both residents of Pandhri village in Chandurbazar tehsil, were washed away in the strong current on the night of June 29. The search operation began on the morning of June 30.

The first question after any security incident isn't "how did this happen." It's "what exactly did the agent do?" That question doesn't have a clean answer in most current agent deployments. Logs exist if someone thought to enable them. They're stored somewhere that may or may not be tamper-resistant. Whether they capture the right level of detail depends on decisions made months before the incident, by people who weren't thinking about incident response at the time.  Latch records every decision before execution. Not in a separate logging service that can be modified or deleted. As part of the policy enforcement flow itself. An action that gets allowed has a record. An action that gets denied has a record. The machine identity, the active policy, the timestamp: captured before anything executes, tied to the enforcement decision that authorized or blocked it.  That pre-execution record is what separates a real audit trail from a reconstruction attempt. When a compromised agent causes damage, the investigation requires knowing what the agent was authorized to do versus what it actually did. Without a record tied to a specific policy at a specific moment, those two things are impossible to separate. @RialoHQ built the audit layer into Latch because agent governance without accountability is access control theater.  Regulators will eventually require exactly this kind of record for autonomous agent deployments handling financial transactions or sensitive data. The enterprises building audit infrastructure now, before it becomes mandatory, will be the ones whose deployments survive the first serious regulatory examination of the agent economy.

#Telangana Jagruthi President K. Kavitha Detained During #Boduppal Protest, Injured Amid Scuffle  #TelanganaJagruthi President K. #KavithaRao was detained by Telangana Police on Thursday while staging a protest in Boduppal, demanding land allotment for Telangana movement activists (Udyamakarulu).  A scuffle broke out as police personnel were taking Kavitha into custody. During the commotion, she reportedly slipped from the officers' grip and fell onto the ground, where large stones were present, sustaining injuries.  The organisation stated that the scuffle intensified, with some police personnel falling on top of Kavitha, causing further injuries. Despite being injured, she was lifted again and taken towards the police vehicle without being given time to recover.  Telangana Jagruthi further stated that amid the continued pushing and jostling, Kavitha also struck the door of the police vehicle, resulting in additional injuries.  The organisation strongly condemned the police action, stating that its president, K. Kavitha, was seriously injured during the detention. However, there has been no official response from the Telangana Police regarding the incident so far.  @RaoKavitha

WATCH: CIA Director John Ratcliffe says the capabilities of frontier AIs are like "digital nuclear weapons".  This comes amid growing concern amongst intelligence officials about dangerous AI capabilities, with Senator Mark Warner, vice-chair of the Senate Intelligence Committee, recently reporting that the NSA’s director, General Joshua Rudd, said that Mythos broke into almost all of their classified systems in hours.  In March, then-Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard testified on the Intelligence Community's assessment of the rapid advance of AI capabilities, stating that humans must remain in control.  And just last week, the leaders of the Five Eyes cybersecurity agencies issued a rare joint statement warning of the growing threat, saying we "must act now".  While today's AIs possess real dangerous capabilities, they are just a stepping stone on the path to artificial superintelligence. Superintelligence would be able to outsmart, outcompete and overpower humans, and AI companies don't have a credible plan to control it.  As officials start to contend with AI risks, it's crucial not to lose sight of where we're heading.

ที่มาร่วมสร้างความทรงจำร่วมกันตลอดทั้ง 2 รอบการแสดง ก่อนจะเดินทางมาส่งท้ายอย่างอบอุ่นที่เวทีล่าสุด ณ Singapore EXPO (Arena @ EXPO) ประเทศสิงคโปร์  งานนี้ บิวกิ้น ได้นำความประทับใจจาก “BILLKIN FEELQUENCY CONCERT PRESENTED BY CP” ที่เคยจัดขึ้นในประเทศไทย มาร้อยเรียงใหม่และเพิ่มเพลงพิเศษ   โดยเพลย์ลิสต์ทั้งหมดถูกร้อยเรียงเพื่อเเฟน ๆ ทุกคน เริ่มต้นเปิดโชว์ด้วยเพลง ‘นับหนึ่ง (From Now On)’, ‘Golden Hour’, ‘รู้งี้เป็นแฟนกันตั้งนานแล้ว (Safe Zone)’ ต่อด้วยเพลง ‘Daily Magic’ ที่มีลูกเล่นการแปรอักษร BILLKIN LOVES SINGAPORE ก่อนจะเพิ่มเอเนอร์จีความสนุกสนานร่วมกันในเพลง ‘Play That Funky Music’ และ ‘I Feel Good’ จากนั้น บิวกิ้น พาผู้ชมเข้าสู่ช่วงเครื่องสายที่เรียบเรียงมาอย่างละเมียดละไม ถ่ายทอดความรู้สึกผ่านเพลง ‘การเดินทางที่สวยงาม (A Beautiful Ride)’, ‘สวยงามเสมอ (Ever-Forever)’ ต่อเนื่องด้วยเพลง ‘You’re My Everything’, ‘หลอกกันทั้งนั้น’, ‘กีดกัน (Skyline)’ และ ‘ชอบตัวเองตอนอยู่กับเธอ (I Like Us)’ ต่อด้วยโชว์ในสไตล์ Broadway Musical และ Jazz Big Band ในเพลง ‘Mr. Everything’, ‘I Just Called To Say I Love You’ และ ‘My Way’  นอกจากนี้ยังมีพาร์ตซึ้งที่ บิวกิ้น ตั้งใจถ่ายทอดความรู้สึก และปรับเพลย์ลิสต์ในแต่ละเมืองให้แตกต่างกันเพื่อมอบเป็นของขวัญให้แฟน ๆ ในแต่ละรอบ โดยที่สิงคโปร์จะเป็นเพลง ‘ยิ้มทั้งน้ำตา (Always)’, ‘กลับมาคบกันเถอะ (Please Please)’ และ ‘All I Ask’ ก่อนจะปรับอารมณ์ให้สดใสในพาร์ตแดนซ์เมดเลย์เพลงฮิต ‘รักแรกพบ (Knock Knock)’, ‘I ไม่ O (IXO)’, ‘Moves Like Jagger’, ‘Bye Bye Bye’, ‘ยิ่งดุยิ่งชอบ’ และ ‘APT.’ ที่กลายเป็นกระแสไวรัล ก่อนเดินทักทายแฟน ๆ อย่างเป็นกันเองทั่วฮอลล์ในเพลง ‘Mr. Everything’ และพาทุกคนเข้าสู่ช่วงท้ายอย่างซาบซึ้งด้วยเพลง  ‘See You Somewhere’, ‘กอดในใจ’ และ ‘Grow With The Flow’ บทเพลงที่ บิวกิ้น เขียนเนื้อร้องเองเพื่อเล่าเรื่องราวการเติบโตผ่านเสียงเพลง  โดยไฮไลต์ช่วงท้ายของเวทีที่สิงค์โปร์ กลายเป็นโมเมนต์ที่แสนอบอุ่น แฟน ๆ ที่ยังไม่อยากให้ค่ำคืนนี้ผ่านพ้นไป ต่างพร้อมใจกันส่งเสียงเรียก “อังกอร์” ดังสนั่นลั่นฮอลล์  ทำเอา บิวกิ้น ทนเสียงเรียกร้องของเเฟน ๆ ไม่ไหว กระโดดขึ้นบนเวทีคว้าไมค์มอบเซอร์ไพรส์พิเศษ ร้องเพลง ‘รักเเรกพบ’ ส่งท้ายโชว์ตามคำเรียกร้อง กลายเป็นโมเมนต์อังกอร์ที่น่ารัก และอบอุ่น ส่งทุกคนกลับบ้านด้วยรอยยิ้ม ปิดท้ายค่ำคืนลงอย่างสวยงาม  และยังมีกิจกรรมพิเศษ “BILLKIN FEELQUENCY MINI LIVE SESSION ONLINE LIVE STREAM” เมื่อวันที่ 25 มิถุนายน 2569 ถ่ายทอดการแสดงสดสุดเอ็กซ์คลูซีฟ พร้อมเซตลิสต์ที่คัดสรรมาเป็นพิเศษ ได้แก่ ‘รักแรกพบ (Knock Knock)’, ‘กลับมาคบกันเถอะ (Please Please)’, ‘ใครจะรู้ (Silent Blue)’, ‘นับหนึ่ง (From Now On)’, ‘Mr. Everything’ และเพลงพิเศษ ‘ย้าย่ายะ’ ต่อเนื่องกับเพลง ‘Golden Hour’, ‘ชอบตัวเองตอนอยู่กับเธอ (I Like Us)’ และ ‘Grow With The Flow’ เปรียบเสมือนการปิดท้ายการเดินทางของ FEELQUENCY อย่างสมบูรณ์แบบ แม้จะไม่ได้พบกันต่อหน้าบนเวที แต่ทุกความรู้สึกยังคงเชื่อมถึงกันผ่านเสียงเพลง  การเดินทางของ “BILLKIN FEELQUENCY TOUR” จึงปิดฉากลงอย่างอบอุ่น พร้อมทิ้งความทรงจำอันสวยงามไว้ในทุกเมืองที่ได้เดินทางไปพบแฟนเพลง และนับเป็นอีกหนึ่งก้าวสำคัญของบิวกิ้นในฐานะศิลปินเดี่ยวที่เติบโตอย่างต่อเนื่อง ก่อนชวนแฟน ๆ ร่วมตั้งตารอผลงานใหม่และอัลบั้มเต็มที่กำลังจะได้ฟังกันในเร็ว ๆ นี้  #BILLKIN_FEELQUENCY_TOUR_IN_SINGAPORE #BILLKIN_FEELQUENCY_TOUR_IN_SEOUL #BILLKIN_FEELQUENCY_TOUR #FEELQUENCYLiveSession #BillkinEntertainment #Bbillkin

Day 2 | SATRC Working Group on Policy, Regulation and Services  Day 2 of the SATRC Working Group on Policy, Regulation and Services focused on finalizing key deliverables under the SATRC Action Plan Phase X (SAP-X), reinforcing regional collaboration on telecommunications policy and regulatory development.  The sessions covered:  * Review and continuation of discussions on the three priority work items * Finalization of questionnaires for the Work Items to support collaborative research and data collection across SATRC member administrations * Consideration and adoption of the consolidated Work Plan of the Working Group * Discussions on the way forward and future collaborative initiatives under SAP-X  The meeting concluded with closing remarks by the Asia-Pacific Telecommunity (APT), the Chair of SATRC, and Dr. Khawar Siddique Khokhar, Chair of the SATRC Working Group on Policy, Regulation and Services. Delegates reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening regional cooperation and advancing harmonized policy and regulatory frameworks for digital transformation across South Asia.  #SATRC #APT #PTA #SATRC2026 #WGPRS #BTRC #BICMA #CAM #TRC #TRAI #USF #MoIT #SCO #Zong #Jazz #Ufone #Telenor #DigitalConnectivity #DigitalSouthAsia #TelecomRegulation #ICTInnovation #TechLeadership #CollaborationForGrowth #GlobalTelecomCommunity

【情報操作・国家主導IO】ウクライナ侵攻で鍛えられたロシアの影響力エコシステムが再びグローバル展開へ——GTIGが構造分析を公開  Google Threat Intelligence Group（GTIG）は、ロシアの影響力工作（IO）エコシステムの包括的な構造分析を公開した。ウクライナ侵攻開始から4年、戦争を契機に急拡大・高度化したプロロシアのIOが、再び欧米・NATO・EU・旧ソ連圏への広域展開へと軸足を移しつつあるという評価が核心だ。  エコシステムは6つのコアコンポーネントから成る。①ロシア政府（政策・ナラティブの源泉）、②国営メディア（RTなど公式プロパガンダ）、③秘密IO（DoppelgangerやSecondary Infektionなど大規模偽情報キャンペーン）、④ハクティビスト（NoName057(16)など、一部GRUと連携）、⑤サイバースパイ＋IO融合操作（UNC4057によるハック＆リーク等）、⑥サードパーティ請負業者（NTC VulkanなどIOインフラ開発の外注先）である。これらは独立して動きながらも相互に強化し合い、露見後も活動を継続する高い持続性を備える。  戦術面では、ジェネレーティブAIをIO全ライフサイクル（企画・リサーチ・コンテンツ生成）に活用する傾向が加速している。社会内の既存の分断に乗じる「ナラティブ共鳴」戦術、メール・SMSを用いた直接拡散、正規メディアに偽装したメディアミミクリも引き続き駆使される。「Operation Overload」などの後継キャンペーンはウクライナ支援を超えた地政学的目標にすでに転用済みであり、今後の大規模選挙・オリンピック・中東情勢等への乗じた活動強化が警戒される。 cloud.google.com/blog/topics…

Hacker enthüllen: Ukraine verlor seit 2022 über 2,4 Millionen Soldaten  Nach Erkenntnissen von Hackergruppen soll die Ukraine seit Kriegsbeginn 2022 über 2,4 Millionen Soldaten verloren haben. Die aus angezapften Datenbanken des Generalstabs, der Rekrutierungszentren TCC, von Krankenhäusern und Leichenhallen stammenden Zahlen zeigen eine verheerende Entwicklung, die von der Kiewer Regierung stets abgestritten wird. Bereits bis August 2025 wurden demnach 1,7 Millionen getötete oder vermißte Soldaten gezählt, im Dezember 2025 überschritt die Verluststatistik die Zwei-Millionen-Marke. Allein im ersten Halbjahr 2026 seien erneut rund 400.000 Mann gefallen – so viele wie im gesamten Jahr 2023. Die durchschnittliche Verlustquote wird in den Dateien auf etwa 500 Soldaten pro Tag beziffert.   Die Datenlecks werden den Hackergruppen PalachPro und NoName057(16) zugeschrieben. PalachPro soll bereits tausende Überwachungskameras in der Ukraine lahmgelegt haben, NoName057(16) zeichnet für großangelegte DDoS-Attacken auf ukrainische Einrichtungen verantwortlich. Die Zahlen, sollten sie zutreffend sein, übertreffen alle bisher öffentlich bekannten Schätzungen bei weitem.   Also solche unglaublichen Verluste an der Ukraine. Und unsere Regierung tut nur, dass sie den tatsächlichen Tod von Menschen unterstützt. Außerdem berauben sie uns, um all das zu finanzieren. So oder so, aber dieser Wahnsinn muss aufhören! So wird es definitiv besser sein, sowohl für uns als auch für die Ukraine.

🇯🇵 Another Japanese Corporate Giant Caught Red-Handed Faking Critical Safety Data... Again!   Chubu Electric Power tampered with seismic data at the Hamaoka nuclear plant. They hid earthquake risks on purpose. Japan’s Nuclear Regulation Authority opened a formal investigation in May last year and in January the company admitted it.  The managers came out publicly, did their bows, delivered the public apology and followed the usual routine.  Then regulators found another 69 violations and this was after the investigation had already begun. The head of the authority, Yamanaka Shinsuke, said the company showed a complete collapse of safety culture and “engineer ethics.” Some commissioners pointed to possible involvement by top management. Japanese citizens are furious and demanding a real crackdown.  The company says it takes the matter “extremely seriously” and apologised again. Insiders admit they still do not grasp the seriousness and have not properly reined in the departments responsible. That should sound very familiar right?  This is not just one bad company. Japanese firms have been caught falsifying data across every major sector for years. The pattern is frequent, cross-industry and long-running.  In 2024, Toyota, Honda, Mazda, Suzuki and Yamaha were caught rigging crash tests, engine power and brake data across 38 models and roughly 5.18 million vehicles.  - Toyota’s subsidiary Daihatsu had been doing it for 30 years across 64 models.  - Kawasaki Heavy faked engine test data for ships and submarines for over 30 years.  - Mitsubishi Electric doctored tests for four decades.  - IHI falsified fuel efficiency data on more than 4,000 engines over 20 years.  - Kobe Steel, JR East and JR Freight.  The list goes on and on, it is not only a disgrace, but it is embarrassing.  Why does it keep happening? Relentless cost and performance pressure in a hyper-competitive market. A rigid hierarchy where underlings stay silent to keep their jobs and penalties so light that a bow on television is basically the only real cost.  Looking at it from China, the difference could not be more obvious.  Chinese manufacturers face intense oversight too, but the focus is on genuine quality, innovation and accountability that actually delivers. Japan’s “precision manufacturing” brand, once world-renowned, is getting hollowed out by systemic rot.  When major corporations manipulate public safety data to meet goals, how long will it take for people to lose faith in the brand?  Japan keeps apologising, but it never seems to fix the culture.  This is another reason why China is taking the lead in the automotive industry, as well as others.

$IONQ   Four of the five Clavis XG models need dedicated fiber for the quantum signal. The fifth one doesn't.  Clavis XG Multiplex, launched June 17 by ID Quantique (an IonQ company), is the newest of five. The portfolio logic is the story.  QKD in one line: encryption keys travel as quantum states of light, any interception disturbs the channel, and both endpoints detect it instantly. Physics, not math assumptions.  The line-up:  → Short Haul, Backbone, Long Haul: point-to-point on dedicated fiber, 60 to 150 km → Hub & Spoke: star deployments, up to 90 km → Multiplex: quantum keys riding the same shared metro fiber as live data (WDM), up to 60 km  Dedicated dark fiber is the hidden line item of every QKD deployment. In metro networks it is scarce and expensive. Multiplex removes it.  What actually sells these boxes is the integration. They interface with the encryptors operators already run: Cisco, Nokia, Ciena, Juniper, Thales, ADVA, Fortinet, Palo Alto. OSI Layers 1 to 3 plus MPLS. ETSI and ITU standards work. Enterprise-grade form factor, maintenance and management, with Clarion KX handling every key from an existing NOC or SOC.  No rip-and-replace. That is the pitch.  Buyers: critical infrastructure, governments, telcos, banks. Anyone holding data with a 5-10+ year shelf life, already exposed to "harvest now, decrypt later," the top quantum concern for 61% of firms in Thales' latest Data Threat Report.  "Making the existing infrastructure more enterprise grade, secure, and QKD cheaper to operate." - @jordan_shapiro , @IonQ_Inc President, Quantum Platform  IonQ's own Florida LambdaRail corridor is the case in point: ~100 quantum-safe miles now launching on a 1,540-mile network. Cost gates every deployment like it. This portfolio attacks the gate.  Security is the part of IonQ's platform that generates revenue today, not in the 2030s.  🔗idquantique.com/quantum-safe… #IonQ #Quantum #QKD

sample_tweets.text: 🌐 Data theft has become one of the most profitable business models in cybercrime.  ShinyHunters didn't become one of the most recognized names by deploying sophisticated malware. Instead, the group repeatedly demonstrated how exposed credentials, cloud misconfigurations, and weak identity security can lead to breaches affecting hundreds of millions of people.  Our latest research explores how ShinyHunters evolved from selling stolen databases to operating large-scale extortion campaigns, the tactics behind some of its most significant operations, and what organizations can learn from them.  Understanding how data is stolen is just as important as understanding how ransomware spreads.  🔗 Download the Report: threatmon.io/the-business-of… #ThreatIntelligence #CyberThreatIntelligence #DataBreach #CyberSecurity #ShinyHunters #ThreatResearch #ThreatMon

In our latest blog, we break down how ClickFix campaigns work, why they're proving so effective, and the practical steps organizations can take to reduce the risk.  Read the full analysis: threatmon.io/clickfix-how-a-… #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #ClickFix #SocialEngineering #CyberThreats

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Anthropic Fable 5 is back!   According to the announcement:  * Designed to tackle more complex tasks with fewer check-ins. * Available for users to try until July 7 using up to 50% of their weekly plan limit. * Consumes usage limits faster than Opus 4.8 due to its higher compute requirements.  As AI models continue to evolve, expect more capable long-running agents, deeper reasoning, and increasingly autonomous workflows, making AI both a stronger productivity tool and a more attractive target for abuse.  💬 Have you tested Fable 5 yet? How does it compare to GPT-5.5, Gemini, Grok, or other frontier models for your workflow?  #DDW #Intelligence #AI #Anthropic

🚗 Ford's AI reality check.  After investing heavily in AI-powered quality control, Ford has reportedly rehired more than 300 experienced engineers after discovering that AI alone couldn't match decades of human expertise in identifying manufacturing and design issues. The veteran engineers are now helping train both younger staff and the AI systems themselves. The strategy has already been linked to improvements in vehicle quality and lower warranty costs.  * AI isn't replacing expertise—it is amplifying it. * Institutional knowledge remains one of the hardest things to automate. * The strongest AI deployments combine human judgment with machine efficiency.  Analyst Note: This isn't a failure of AI. It's a reminder that AI is only as effective as the experience, data, and domain knowledge behind it. Organizations treating AI as a replacement rather than a force multiplier will continue to face similar challenges.  #AI #Ford #Manufacturing #Automation #DDW

🇮🇳  Indian Logistics Firm Allegedly Exposed in Fresh Data Leak  A threat actor is advertising a fresh database allegedly belonging to shreetransport.co, an India-based transportation and logistics company.  According to the forum post: * The seller claims the dataset includes employee payroll and banking information. * Allegedly exposed fields include bank account numbers, IFSC codes, bank IDs, branch IDs, cheque details, salary information, payment dates, deductions, leave records, voucher IDs, and transport-related identifiers. * The post specifically highlights that banking information is included in the leak.  Analyst Note: The authenticity of the dataset has not been independently verified. If confirmed, exposure of payroll and banking information could significantly increase the risk of financial fraud, business email compromise, payroll diversion, and targeted phishing against affected employees.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #India

🤖 Navoy AI Travel Platform Allegedly Breached  A threat actor claims to have breached Navoy, a San Francisco-based AI travel platform, and is offering the allegedly stolen database for download.  According to the forum post: * The actor claims to have obtained the platform's entire user database. * The post states the dataset contains 749 user records. * Allegedly exposed fields include email addresses, first and last names, last sign-in timestamps, account roles, email verification status, profile images, authentication providers, Google IDs, and account creation/update timestamps. * The threat actor claims the compromise resulted from easily exploitable vulnerabilities within the platform.  Analyst Note: These claims have not been independently verified. While the reported record count is relatively small, exposed identity and authentication metadata could facilitate phishing, account takeover attempts, and targeted social engineering if confirmed.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #AI

🚨 Practices / Practisis POS SaaS Database Allegedly Offered for Sale  A threat actor is advertising what is claimed to be a 1.47 TB database belonging to the Practices/Practisis POS ecosystem, which includes Practices.net, Practisis.com, PracticesDora.com, and Dora.de.  According to the forum post: * The platform is described as a POS/SaaS provider serving approximately 2,000–3,000 clients. * The seller claims the dataset is approximately 1.47 TB in size. * The post also alleges access to cloud backup infrastructure, including S3 bucket credentials, with a screenshot claiming the data was accessed as recently as July 1, 2026. * Additional contents are reportedly available but hidden behind the forum reply mechanism.  Analyst Note: These claims have not been independently verified. If authentic, exposure of a POS SaaS provider and its backup infrastructure could impact thousands of downstream businesses, potentially exposing customer, transaction, and operational data while increasing the risk of supply chain compromise.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #DataBreach

🇲🇽 ISSSTE Historical Database with 4M+ Records Allegedly Offered for Sale  A threat actor is advertising what is claimed to be a historical database belonging to Mexico's ISSSTE (Instituto de Seguridad y Servicios Sociales de los Trabajadores del Estado).  According to the forum post: * The seller claims the database contains more than 4 million historical records. * Allegedly exposed fields include ISSSTE and NSS numbers, CURP, RFC, payroll and branch information, office numbers, names, parents' names, enrollment details, appointment types, salary adjustments, pension plans, salary payments, and total compensation. * The dataset is being offered for sale with a negotiable price.  Analyst Note: The authenticity of the dataset has not been independently verified. If genuine, the exposure could affect current and former public-sector employees and retirees, creating significant risks of identity theft, financial fraud, targeted phishing, and other forms of abuse due to the sensitivity and volume of the alleged records.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Mexico

🇧🇷 Alfa.bet.br Source Code Allegedly Offered for Sale on a Dark Web Forum  A threat actor is advertising what is claimed to be the complete source code of Alfa.bet.br, a Brazil-based online betting platform.  According to the forum post: * The seller claims to possess the full source code and project directory structure. * A file tree has been shared publicly as proof of possession. * The actor states the source code is available exclusively in exchange for Monero (XMR), with an asking price of 5 XMR. * The post also claims the organization was previously notified before the source code was offered for sale.  Analyst Note: The authenticity of the source code has not been independently verified. If legitimate, exposure of the application's source code could increase the risk of vulnerability discovery, infrastructure compromise, credential exposure, and future attacks against the platform.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Brazil

😂 Internet legend confirmed.  WinRAR just thanked paying customers for making version 7.23 possible.  * After decades of "using the free trial forever," WinRAR leaned into one of the internet's oldest memes. * The company officially released WinRAR 7.23 with bug fixes and improvements. * Their announcement instantly went viral, proving they know exactly how the internet sees them.  WinRAR has quietly remained one of the most widely used archive utilities in enterprise and consumer environments. Sometimes the best marketing strategy is embracing the joke instead of fighting it.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #WinRAR

⚖️ A new antitrust lawsuit accuses Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron of coordinating DRAM supply reductions to inflate RAM prices.  * The lawsuit alleges the three companies control ~90% of the global DRAM market. * Plaintiffs claim production was intentionally shifted toward AI-focused HBM memory while conventional DRAM supply was restricted. * If proven, the alleged conduct could have contributed to years of rising memory prices across PCs, servers, and consumer electronics.  These are allegations made in a newly filed U.S. class-action lawsuit. The claims have not been proven, and the defendants have not yet been found liable.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Memory

🇲🇽 Hospital Angeles Patient Database Allegedly Leaked on a Dark Web Forum  A threat actor has published what is claimed to be data associated with Hospital Angeles, one of Mexico's largest private healthcare networks.  According to the forum post: * The dataset is presented as a patient database. * The leak was posted publicly on June 29, 2026. * A download link is provided, suggesting the dataset has been released rather than offered for sale.  Analyst Note: The authenticity of the dataset has not been independently verified. If confirmed, the exposure could involve sensitive healthcare information, creating significant privacy, identity theft, and regulatory risks for affected patients.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Mexico

🇮🇶 Alayen Iraqi University Doctors Database Allegedly Leaked on a Dark Web Forum  A threat actor has published what is claimed to be a database containing records associated with Alayen Iraqi University (alayen.edu.iq).  According to the forum post: * The dataset is described as containing doctors' records. * Sample fields shown include names, job titles, usernames, passwords, and dates. * A sample of the alleged database has been shared publicly.  Analyst Note: The authenticity of the dataset has not been independently verified. If confirmed, the exposure could result in unauthorized access to university accounts and increase the risk of credential abuse and targeted phishing against academic staff.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Iraq

🇲🇽 Mexico's Secretariat of the Navy (SEMAR) Database Allegedly Offered for Sale on a Dark Web Forum  A threat actor is advertising what is claimed to be a database belonging to Mexico's Secretariat of the Navy (SEMAR).  According to the forum post: * The dataset allegedly contains approximately 3.2 million personnel records covering the 2025–2026 period. * The seller claims the data includes personal information such as names, surnames, and CURP identifiers sourced from multiple Mexican states. * The post further alleges the database contains military ranks, specialties, regional assignments, operational information, fleet details, and payroll-related data.  Analyst Note: The authenticity of the dataset has not been independently verified. If confirmed, the exposure could have significant implications for national security and the privacy of military personnel.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Mexico

🚨 Our research team at FalconFeeds has identified a #TransparentTribe (APT36) campaign exploiting the 2026 #NEET paper leak controversy as a social engineering lure.  Victims are tricked into opening a weaponized ZIP archive that deploys a Go-based Windows backdoor. Our investigation confirms the malware as StealthServer (the Windows counterpart of DeskRAT) through live WebSocket C2 communications, protocol-level validation, dedicated IDS signature matches, and infrastructure overlap with previously documented Transparent Tribe operations.  The campaign underscores how high-profile national events are rapidly weaponized for cyber espionage targeting Indian entities.

sample_tweets.text: Oh yeah? You're a "hacker"?   Prove it. Hack the electrical grid and give yourself infinite electricity, then use your infinite electricity to hack Bitcoin and give yourself unlimited Bitcoins   Then hack the airport and give yourself 999999 airplanes and fly around the world

tl;dr really effective malware multi-staged, multiple programming languages, use as many dependencies as possible. AI making this easier to do. AVs struggling  Historically, in regards to malware development, the end goal was minimalism. It was in your best interest to strip as many dependencies, shred the file size down, and make it position independent.  I think, as of ... now ... we need to take a different approach.  I think instead of stripping binaries, we (Red Team, Threat Emulation, malware developers) should intentionally introduce dependencies.  I have witnesses two unique things in the malware landscape since the AI boom.  1. Increase in malware slop. I continue to see stagers which contain notes in them. This is not intentional and this does not "trick" the analyst. This is a colossal mistake on the malware developers part. However, despite it being slop, AI has made malware more diverse. I am seeing more and more malware in Lua, Node JS (including SEA and nexe), Java, and Python. I am seeing more and more malware doing inter-process communication across multiple programming languages. Of course all of these have existed prior to AI, but I am seeing an explosion in these languages. This also has resulted in malware researchers creating new tools to combat this malware diversity.  2. Anti-malware services struggling. When I encounter a binary that is a Node JS SEA blob (Electron JS .exe, self-contained using SEA), which extracts a .JS payload, which uses obfuscated Java or heavily obfuscated Lua, all of these languages require a VM (PVM, LVM, JVM, whatever) for interpretation. Thus, with heavy obfuscation and multistaging, static analysis fails and the heavy abstraction makes it difficult for traditional hooking or minifilters to be effective, in essence there is too much noise. Many of these payloads with heavy dependencies easily avoid static analysis and even some emulation systems because they fail to account for the necessary dependencies which are required to emulate it correctly.  pic maybe related idk

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-07-02 19:42 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 사이버 위협 환경은 AI Agent를 활용한 공격 자동화가 실전 단계에 진입하며, 정찰부터 데이터 암호화까지 전 과정을 자율적으로 수행하는 공격이 관찰되고 있습니다. 특히 LLM의 환각(Hallucination) 현상을 악용한 'Phantom Squatting'과 같은 공급망 공격이 증가하고 있으며, ClickFix와 같은 고도화된 사회공학적 기법이 악성코드 배포의 표준으로 자리 잡고 있습니다. 또한, 취약점 연구자를 타겟으로 한 위장 PoC 코드 배포 등 공격자의 타겟팅이 더욱 정교해지고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **JADEPUFFER (Sysdig):** Langflow RCE 취약점을 악용하여 AI Agent 기반의 데이터베이스 랜섬웨어 공격을 자율적으로 수행.
*   **INC & Lynx (TheHackerNews/BleepingComputer):** FortiBleed 캠페인을 통해 탈취한 Fortinet 자격 증명을 활용하여 네트워크 침투 및 랜섬웨어 공격 수행.
*   **TAG-182 (Recorded Future):** MarkiRAT 악성코드를 사용하여 이란 내 타겟 대상 사이버 감시 및 정보 탈취 수행.
*   **Scattered Spider (BleepingComputer/Krebs):** 주요 인프라 및 기업 대상 데이터 유출 및 랜섬웨어 공격, 최근 핵심 조직원 미국 인도 및 기소.
*   **Kimsuky (이스트시큐리티):** 보안 담당자를 대상으로 개인정보 유출을 사칭한 스피어 피싱 공격 수행.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **Settra:** 한국 산업장비 제조 대기업의 해외 법인 데이터 유출 주장 (AhnLab ASEC).
*   **공급망 공격:** Chai.js 플러그인으로 위장한 악성 npm 패키지 `chai-as-init` 배포 (이스트시큐리티).
*   **APT 공격:** 2026년 5월 국내 타겟 APT 공격 동향 보고서 발행, 지속적인 은밀한 표적 공격 확인 (AhnLab ASEC).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-45659:** Microsoft SharePoint RCE, CISA KEV 등재 및 활발히 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2026-43503:** Ubuntu DirtyClone Kernel Flaw, 긴급 패치 권고.
*   **CVE-2026-4372:** Hugging Face Transformers RCE, AI 모델 로딩 시 악성 config.json으로 시스템 장악 가능.
*   **CVE-2026-44937:** Rancher Fleet Webhook RCE/DoS, 설정 미흡 시 악용 가능.
*   **CVE-2026-8147:** MLflow Trace API 권한 우회, 인증 활성화 환경에서도 취약.

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **CVE:**
    *   CVE-2026-45659 ([SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/cisa-warns-of-actively-exploited-microsoft-sharepoint-vulnerability/))
    *   CVE-2026-43503 ([YouTube/Jadiri79](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ckRxL65jCmQ))
    *   CVE-2026-4372 ([X/AIAshokSingh](https://twitter.com/AIAshokSingh/status/2072629357143842944))
    *   CVE-2026-44937 ([X/UpwindMDR](https://twitter.com/UpwindMDR/status/2072627950038478994))
    *   CVE-2026-8147 ([X/UpwindMDR](https://twitter.com/UpwindMDR/status/2072627596609704427))
*   **도메인/해시:** 이번 수집 데이터 내 특정 도메인 및 파일 해시 값은 공개되지 않음.

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **SharePoint 패치:** CISA KEV에 등재된 CVE-2026-45659에 대해 즉각적인 보안 업데이트 적용 및 외부 노출 최소화.
*   **AI/ML 환경 보안:** Hugging Face 등 외부 모델 로딩 시 신뢰할 수 없는 `config.json` 및 모델 파일 검증 프로세스 강화.
*   **자격 증명 관리:** Fortinet 등 네트워크 장비의 자격 증명 재설정 및 MFA(다중 인증) 강제 적용 (FortiBleed 캠페인 대응).

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수:** 8/10
*   **근거:** AI Agent를 활용한 공격 자동화가 현실화되었고, CISA KEV 등재 취약점(SharePoint)이 활발히 악용되고 있어 기업 인프라에 대한 즉각적인 위협이 매우 높음.
